stem: A manufacturing study measures defect rate across 4 factorys, each containing multiple production lines. factory A has 8 observations with mean 70.4 % (within-factory SD = 10.0). factory B has 15 observations with mean 58.3 % (within-factory SD = 7.9). factory C has 20 observations with mean 51.2 % (within-factory SD = 8.9). factory D has 15 observations with mean 42.5 % (within-factory SD = 3.5). The between-factory standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 5.3 and 13.4 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true defect rate for factory A, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-factory variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for factory A: 68.1 % (shrunk from raw 70.4). Range [65.2, 69.3] as between-factory SD varies from 5.3 to 13.4. Pooled mean: 53.4.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 53.4 % (weighted average of 4 factory means).
Shrinkage factor for factory A: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/8). At σ_between=8.9: B = 0.864.
Interval [65.2, 69.3] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (70.4) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (53.4) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
